reports.title: Hotel switch from IGI airport
reports.desc: Driver claims hotel closed/full, takes you to commission hotel paying him 30–50%.

reports.title: Fake 'Government Tourist Office' in CP/Paharganj
reports.desc: Sells fake train tickets, overpriced tours. Real one is at 88 Janpath Road.

reports.title: New Delhi Railway Station 'tourist office' upstairs
reports.desc: Real IRCTC International Tourist Bureau is on 1st floor. Touts redirect you to fake one.

reports.title: Fake police in Paharganj
reports.desc: Plainclothes 'officers' demand passport check + on-spot fine. Real police don't fine on the spot.

reports.title: Auto refusing meter / inflated fares
reports.desc: Use Uber/Ola — they're 30–60% cheaper than tourist auto rates.

reports.title: Pickpocketing in Chandni Chowk metro
reports.desc: Yellow line during rush hour is hotspot. Front pocket only, zipped bag.

reports.title: Bird poop / shirt stain distraction in CP
reports.desc: Two-person team. Step back, don't let anyone touch you.

reports.title: Hotel Switch / 'Your hotel burned down'
reports.desc: Taxi/auto driver claims your booked hotel is closed, burned, overbooked, or in a 'riot zone'. Drives you to a partner hotel paying 30–50% commission. Currently most active at IGI Airport T3 (after midnight arrivals) and New Delhi Railway Station.

reports.title: Sakha — women-only taxis run by women
reports.desc: Social enterprise; all-women drivers from marginalised communities. >1M safe rides reported. ₹200–600 city rides. Book via app or call.

reports.title: Delhi Metro is genuinely safe for solo women
reports.desc: Women-only first coach. CCTV on all platforms. Late-night service 11pm. Avoid sketchy autos by taking metro to closest station.

reports.title: Pickpocketing on local trains & at CST
reports.desc: Mumbai's #1 tourist crime. Front pocket, zipped bag, no phone in back pocket.

reports.title: Fake police at Colaba / Gateway
reports.desc: Demand 'foreigner check' + on-spot fine. Demand to go to police station instead.

reports.title: Gem & carpet shops in Colaba
reports.desc: Same export scam, slightly less aggressive than Jaipur.

reports.title: Tourist boat overcharge to Elephanta
reports.desc: Official Elephanta ferry is ₹220–270 from Gateway. Touts quote ₹500–800.

reports.title: Photo + 'small donation' at Gateway
reports.desc: Photographer gives 'free' photo, then asks for ₹500.

reports.title: Hotel switch from airport
reports.desc: Less common than Delhi but still happens at international arrivals after midnight.

reports.title: Bottled water tampering near beaches
reports.desc: Check seal, prefer chain stores.

reports.title: Hotel Switch / 'Your hotel burned down'
reports.desc: Taxi/auto driver claims your booked hotel is closed, burned, overbooked, or in a 'riot zone'. Drives you to a partner hotel paying 30–50% commission. Currently most active at IGI Airport T3 (after midnight arrivals) and New Delhi Railway Station.

reports.title: Mumbai Local women-only compartments
reports.desc: First-class ladies coach is the safest way to travel. Off-peak hours quietest. Avoid 8–10am and 5–8pm rush in general compartment.

reports.title: Priyadarshini Taxi — women-driver service
reports.desc: Verified women drivers. Pre-book via phone or Uber Women-only Bike Rides via app. ₹150–400 typical city ride.

reports.title: Gem export scam (the legendary one)
reports.desc: Friendly local → 'family jeweller' → 'export gems for profit' → glass beads. Lose lakhs.

reports.title: Textile / block print 'family workshop'
reports.desc: Driver commissions you to specific shop. Prices 5–10x normal Bapu Bazaar rates.

reports.title: 'Friendly student' approaches at Hawa Mahal
reports.desc: Sets up the gem scam. Walk away from anyone unprompted.

reports.title: Amer Fort 'closed' / wrong gate
reports.desc: Touts redirect you to a paid 'alternate viewing point' = commission shop.

reports.title: Auto charging by 'tourist rate'
reports.desc: Use Ola/Uber/Rapido. Auto from station to old city = ₹150 by app vs ₹500 quoted.

reports.title: Fake palace tour packages
reports.desc: WhatsApp groups offering ₹500 'all monuments tour' drop you at commission shops.

reports.title: Elephant ride at Amer overcharge & welfare issue
reports.desc: Govt rate is fixed (~₹1,100). Touts charge ₹3,000+. Also: ethical concerns documented by PETA India.

reports.title: Hotel Switch / 'Your hotel burned down'
reports.desc: Taxi/auto driver claims your booked hotel is closed, burned, overbooked, or in a 'riot zone'. Drives you to a partner hotel paying 30–50% commission. Currently most active at IGI Airport T3 (after midnight arrivals) and New Delhi Railway Station.

reports.title: Pink City Rickshaw Co — women drivers
reports.desc: Women drivers from low-income backgrounds. Heritage walks + day rentals. ₹400–800/day. Book at official kiosk near Hawa Mahal or call.

reports.title: 'Taj Mahal closed today' / fake holiday
reports.desc: Touts near gates. Taj is closed only on FRIDAYS — verify yourself at the ticket counter.

reports.title: Fake guides at Taj Mahal
reports.desc: Demand ₹2,000–₹5,000 after free 'tour'. Hire only ASI-licensed guides with photo ID at the counter.

reports.title: Marble inlay / gem export scam
reports.desc: Drivers commission you to shops claiming 'family workshop'. Same scam as Jaipur — overpriced fakes.

reports.title: Auto/taxi inflated fares from Cantt station
reports.desc: Use prepaid taxi at station OR Uber/Ola. Quoted rates are 3–5x real.

reports.title: Photographer at Taj demanding fees
reports.desc: Approach you with 'free photo' offer, then demand ₹500–₹2,000 per print.

reports.title: Shoe-cover fee scam
reports.desc: Free shoe covers come with your Taj ticket. Don't pay anyone selling them.

reports.title: Hotel Switch / 'Your hotel burned down'
reports.desc: Taxi/auto driver claims your booked hotel is closed, burned, overbooked, or in a 'riot zone'. Drives you to a partner hotel paying 30–50% commission. Currently most active at IGI Airport T3 (after midnight arrivals) and New Delhi Railway Station.

reports.title: Fake Government Tourist Office
reports.desc: Fake offices with 'Govt. of India Tourism' signage sell inflated tour packages, fake train tickets, or non-existent hotel bookings. The real India Tourism office is at 88 Janpath Road, Delhi. Real IRCTC International Tourist Bureau is on the 1st floor of New Delhi Railway Stat…

reports.title: Buy Taj tickets online at asi.payumoney.com
reports.desc: Avoids the 200+ touts at the gate selling fake or marked-up tickets. Foreign tourist ₹1,100, India ₹50. QR scan at gate. Skip the queue too.

reports.title: Auto rickshaw refusing meter
reports.desc: Most rampant scam in Bangalore. ALWAYS use Rapido/Uber/Namma Yatri (official BMTC-backed app).

reports.title: Fake driver at airport claiming to be your Uber/Ola
reports.desc: Verify license plate before getting in.

reports.title: Fake brand stores in Commercial Street
reports.desc: Counterfeit Apple/Samsung accessories. Buy electronics at authorised stores only.

reports.title: ATM skimming in standalone street ATMs
reports.desc: Use only HDFC/ICICI/SBI/Axis branch ATMs.

reports.title: Pickpocketing at Majestic during festivals
reports.desc: Especially during Dussehra, Diwali.

reports.title: Fake job/visa scam targeting foreign workers
reports.desc: Recent rise — affects African and SE Asian tourists.

reports.title: Pre-paid Taxi Diversion / Fake Driver
reports.desc: After paying at the official prepaid counter, driver claims he doesn't know your address, the road is closed, or there's a strike. Detours to commission shops or drops you at wrong hotel. Variant: a tout at the airport claims to be your booked Uber/Ola driver.

reports.title: Bird Poop / Stained Shirt Distraction
reports.desc: Stranger points out 'something' on your shirt; accomplice 'helps clean' while picking your pocket of phone/wallet.

reports.title: Uber Women — women drivers, women riders only
reports.desc: Launched Dec 2024. Book via Uber app. Women-only driver matched with women-only rider. ₹50–200 typical. Available 24/7 across the city.

reports.title: Namma Metro is safe — women-only coach
reports.desc: First coach reserved for women on every train. CCTV throughout. Stations well-lit and staffed until 11pm. Best way to skip evening traffic safely.

reports.title: Auto rickshaw extreme overcharge
reports.desc: Chennai autos are notorious. ALWAYS use Uber/Ola/Rapido.

reports.title: Medical tourism middleman scam
reports.desc: Fake 'patient coordinator' offers cheap surgery at top hospital, takes advance, disappears. Book directly with hospital international patient desk.

reports.title: Marina Beach hawker overcharge
reports.desc: ₹100 corn for tourists vs ₹30 for locals. Fix price first.

reports.title: Pickpocketing in T Nagar (Pondy Bazaar)
reports.desc: Especially Saturdays. Crowded shopping district.

reports.title: Fake gold/silver shops
reports.desc: Especially Sowcarpet & Mint Street. Buy only from BIS-hallmarked retailers (Tanishq, GRT, Joyalukkas).

reports.title: Airport pre-paid taxi diversion
reports.desc: Less common than Delhi but happens.

reports.title: Auto/Taxi Meter Refusal & Inflation
reports.desc: Driver says meter is 'broken', quotes flat fare 3–10x normal. In Mumbai it's rarer because of strict meter culture. In Bangalore mostly auto drivers at airport/railway.

reports.title: Train Ticket / IRCTC Scam
reports.desc: Near railway stations, agents sell 'confirmed tickets' that are fake or for slow local trains sold as fast express. Variant: tout claims 'your train is cancelled' and herds you to a fake ticket counter to sell flights/tours at 5x rates.

reports.title: Pre-paid taxi counter at Chennai Airport
reports.desc: Government counter inside airport gives fixed rates with receipt. Avoids the touts outside. ₹400–600 to most city locations. Show receipt + driver ID match before getting in.

reports.title: Chennai Metro — clean, safe, women-friendly
reports.desc: Women-only first coach. CCTV throughout. Late-night service until 11pm. ₹10–50 fare. Way safer + faster than autos for cross-city trips.

reports.title: Pickpocketing on Kolkata Metro & at Howrah
reports.desc: Front pocket, zipped bag. Especially on Howrah-Dum Dum line during rush.

reports.title: Fake porter overcharge at Howrah
reports.desc: Real porters wear red shirt + metal armband with number. Pre-fix price: ₹50–₹100/bag.

reports.title: Kalighat 'priest' demanding offering
reports.desc: ₹500–₹5,000 demanded. Same as Varanasi pattern. Walk away firmly.

reports.title: Fake brand stores in New Market
reports.desc: Counterfeit watches, perfumes, leather.

reports.title: Auto/taxi refusing meter or quoting flat
reports.desc: Use Uber/Ola. Yellow Ambassador taxis often refuse short distances.

reports.title: Hotel switch scam
reports.desc: Less common than Delhi but happens at Howrah.

reports.title: Auto/Taxi Meter Refusal & Inflation
reports.desc: Driver says meter is 'broken', quotes flat fare 3–10x normal. In Mumbai it's rarer because of strict meter culture. In Bangalore mostly auto drivers at airport/railway.

reports.title: Train Ticket / IRCTC Scam
reports.desc: Near railway stations, agents sell 'confirmed tickets' that are fake or for slow local trains sold as fast express. Variant: tout claims 'your train is cancelled' and herds you to a fake ticket counter to sell flights/tours at 5x rates.

reports.title: Yellow Ambassador taxis run honest meters
reports.desc: The iconic yellow taxis here mostly run meters honestly — better than autos. ₹30 base + ₹15/km. Most drivers are decent and Kolkata is generally safe for solo women in daytime.

reports.title: Kolkata Metro — oldest, cheapest, safest
reports.desc: ₹5–25 fare across the city. Women-only first coach. Crowded at peak hours but safe. Quickest way to avoid Park Street traffic at evenings.

reports.title: Fake priest forced tilak/thread + 'donation'
reports.desc: Ties thread before you can refuse, demands ₹500–₹10,000. Hands in pockets, firm 'no'.

reports.title: Photography extortion at Manikarnika (cremation ghat)
reports.desc: Photo strictly forbidden anyway, but fake 'caretakers' threaten heavy fines if you have a camera. Don't take photos there at all — out of respect & safety.

reports.title: Boat overcharge for sunrise / Aarti
reports.desc: Quoted ₹3,000–₹5,000; real rate is ₹500–₹800 for a shared boat, ₹1,500–₹2,500 private.

reports.title: Fake guide claiming to be 'temple authority'
reports.desc: At Kashi Vishwanath, asks for 'special darshan fee' = pocket money.

reports.title: Drugged tea / lassi from random shops
reports.desc: Bhang lassi shops sometimes much stronger than declared. Tourists hospitalised.

reports.title: Hotel switch from station
reports.desc: Cyclo/auto driver claims hotel closed → commission guesthouse.

reports.title: Shoe theft at temples
reports.desc: Wear cheap sliders. Use the official locker.

reports.title: Hotel Switch / 'Your hotel burned down'
reports.desc: Taxi/auto driver claims your booked hotel is closed, burned, overbooked, or in a 'riot zone'. Drives you to a partner hotel paying 30–50% commission. Currently most active at IGI Airport T3 (after midnight arrivals) and New Delhi Railway Station.

reports.title: Stay near Assi Ghat, not Dashashwamedh
reports.desc: Quieter, more solo-women friendly, less hustle. Walk north to main ghats during day. Multiple verified women-friendly guesthouses. Avoid alleys after 10pm — they're confusing.

reports.title: Airport taxi overcharge on arrival
reports.place: Lal Bahadur Shastri Airport, arrivals exit
reports.desc: Quoted ₹2,000–₹2,500 for a ride worth ₹800–₹950. Touts swarm you the moment you exit. Use the prepaid counter inside the terminal or pre-book Ola/Uber. Sedan to ghats = ₹800–₹950 fixed.

reports.title: "Your hotel is closed/burned down" taxi lie
reports.desc: Driver insists your booked hotel is flooded, shut, or changed and takes you to a commission hotel instead. Your hotel is NOT closed. Call ahead to verify and book Ola from the station.

reports.title: Boat ride mid-river hostage
reports.desc: Quoted ₹200–₹300 per person for a sunrise boat ride. Boatman stops mid-river and demands ₹2,000–₹5,000 to "complete the tour," refusing to return until paid. Fair private boat = ₹1,200–₹2,000 total for a group. Agree on TOTAL fare before boarding — not per person, not per hour.

reports.title: Manikarnika wood donation gang
reports.desc: Men claim to be volunteers helping poor families cremate their dead. Pressure you to donate ₹300+ per kg of wood. It's a gang — money never reaches any family. Anyone can stand and watch freely. Politely walk away.

reports.title: "No photography license" fake fine at cremation ghat
reports.desc: Man approaches claiming you need a paid license to take photos and threatens police. There is no such license. Don't pay. Walk away. (Photography here is disrespectful regardless.)

reports.title: Fake Kashi Vishwanath darshan website
reports.desc: Fake sites like kashivishwanath.in charge for Sugam Darshan, Rudrabhishek and Aarti bookings — especially during Sawan when the official site suspends bookings. ₹10 lakh extracted from hundreds of devotees. Book ONLY via shrikashivishwanath.org.

reports.title: Locker shop prasad pressure near KV Temple
reports.place: Vishwanath Gali (lane to Kashi Vishwanath Temple)
reports.desc: Shops offer to store your phone and shoes before temple entry. Once stored, they pressure you to buy overpriced prasad (₹150+ per basket vs ₹20 actual). Free lockers are available INSIDE the temple corridor, provided by the KV Temple Trust.

reports.title: Fake sadhu tilak then aggressive donation demand
reports.desc: Man in saffron puts a tilak on your forehead without asking, then demands ₹500–₹2,000 — and becomes aggressive or "curses" you if you refuse. Genuine tilak donations are ₹10–₹20 and always agreed first. Don't engage unsolicited "priests."

reports.title: Boat ride overcharge on Lake Pichola
reports.desc: Official rates posted at City Palace ticket counter. Ignore touts at the ghat.

reports.title: Gem & miniature painting export scam
reports.desc: Same as Jaipur. 'Family workshop' = commission. Walk away.

reports.title: Fake heritage 'haveli' bookings
reports.desc: Several scam websites in 2025 — book via Booking.com/MMT only.

reports.title: Auto refusing meter from station
reports.desc: Use Uber/Ola — Udaipur has good coverage now.

reports.title: Fake guide at City Palace
reports.desc: Hire only from official counter.

reports.title: Sunset Point camel/horse ride overcharge
reports.desc: Fix price before mounting.

reports.title: Gem & Carpet Export Scam
reports.desc: Friendly local introduces you to a 'family jeweller'. You're told you can 'export' gems duty-free and resell abroad for huge profit; they ship to your country. Stones are worthless glass/synthetic. Variant: a fake government 'official' calls later demanding fines.

reports.title: Fake Priest / Tilak / Forced Blessing
reports.desc: A 'pandit' ties a thread (kalava) on your wrist or applies tilak before you can refuse, then chants a 'blessing' and demands ₹500–₹10,000 'donation'. Most aggressive at Manikarnika and Dashashwamedh ghats in Varanasi.

reports.title: Udaipur is the safest big tourist city in India
reports.desc: Consistently rated safest big tourist city for solo women. Walking the Old City alone after dark is fine. Locals genuinely helpful, not pushy. Stay near Lake Pichola for best experience.

reports.title: Drug possession setup (Old Manali / Kasol nearby)
reports.desc: Charas offered → 'cop' demands bribe. NDPS Act jail risk. Refuse all offers.

reports.title: Bike/scooter rental damage scam
reports.desc: Photo before+after, never leave passport, use known agencies.

reports.title: Taxi cartel rates to Rohtang/Solang
reports.desc: Fixed by union; high but unavoidable. Hotel can sometimes negotiate.

reports.title: Paragliding operator overcharge / no insurance
reports.desc: Use only HP Tourism-licensed operators. Verify pilot license.

reports.title: Honeymoon package fraud online
reports.desc: Fake Instagram/WhatsApp packages with advance UPI demand. Lose ₹20K–₹1L.

reports.title: Photographer at Hidimba Temple / Mall Road
reports.desc: 'Free photo with traditional dress' → demands ₹500–₹2,000.

reports.title: Drug Possession Setup (Goa)
reports.desc: Local offers drugs at a beach/party; once accepted/handed over, accomplice 'cop' arrives, demands large bribe to avoid arrest. Punishment for narcotics in India is severe — even small amounts = years in jail.

reports.title: Old Manali is the solo-traveller hub
reports.place: Across the river from Mall Road
reports.desc: Cafe culture, hostels with solo women travellers, walking-friendly. Way safer than the touristy Mall Road. Stay at Hosteller / Zostel / Madpackers — all women-friendly chains.

reports.title: Fake houseboat / backwater booking online
reports.desc: Lookalike Kerala Tourism websites. Pay advance, no boat. Book via keralatourism.org or trusted operator (Spice Coast, Lakes & Lagoons).

reports.title: Auto/tuk-tuk overcharge in Fort Kochi
reports.desc: Use Uber/Ola/Rapido — strong coverage.

reports.title: Fake antique / spice 'discount' shops in Jew Town
reports.desc: Inflated prices for 'genuine' spices and 'antiques'. Bargain hard or buy from chain stores.

reports.title: Chinese fishing net 'demonstration fee'
reports.desc: Operators ask ₹500–₹1,000 to 'pull the net'. Fixed local rate is ₹100–₹200.

reports.title: Cruise booking scam (Cordelia)
reports.desc: Fake websites. Book only via cordeliacruises.com or authorised agents.

reports.title: Fake Ayurveda spa packages
reports.desc: Some 'Ayurvedic clinics' have no licensed doctors. Check NABH-accreditation.

reports.title: Beach Shack & Water Sports Overcharge
reports.desc: Menus without prices, jet ski/parasail operators charging 5–10x and damaging equipment to demand 'damage' fees. Renting bikes/scooters with fake damage claims.

reports.title: Cordelia / Cruise Booking Scam
reports.desc: Fake Cordelia / Costa websites take advance for cruises that never sail. Surged in 2025 per Goa MP's statement in Rajya Sabha.

reports.title: Kochi Metro + ferry combo for safe travel
reports.desc: Metro covers Ernakulam side. Public ferry to Fort Kochi from High Court Junction is ₹4 + safe + scenic. Avoids touristy auto overcharging by 5–10x.

reports.title: Fort Kochi homestays are safest accommodation
reports.desc: Verified women-friendly homestays in colonial heritage area. Owner-run, walking distance to all sights, ₹1,500–3,500/night. Way better than the mainland hotels for solo women.

reports.title: Taxi flat-rate scam from Shibuya
reports.desc: Driver offers 'flat rate' to your hotel but charges 3× the meter. Always insist on the meter or use official Tokyo taxis (JPN Taxi app).

reports.title: Rickshaw overcharging in Asakusa
reports.desc: Jinrikisha (rickshaw) riders quote 500 yen, bill ¥5,000 per person after the ride. Agree on price and duration in writing beforehand.

reports.title: Fake 'English students' near Harajuku
reports.desc: Approach claiming to be English students who need practice. Leads to expensive restaurant or bar — bill split between you and 'host'.

reports.title: Bar tout harassment in Roppongi
reports.desc: Men outside bars offer 'free drinks' — bill arrives for thousands of yen. Avoid bars with persistent touts on the street.

reports.title: Pickpocketing on Yamanote Line rush hour
reports.desc: Morning rush (7–9 AM) on the loop line is a prime window. Use women-only carriages (first carriage, pink signage) and keep bags in front.

reports.title: Fake monk donation request in Shinjuku
reports.desc: Robed men approach, hand you a charm, then demand large donation. Legitimate monks do not solicit tourists on streets.

reports.title: Tuk-tuk 'closed temple' scam near Grand Palace
reports.desc: Tuk-tuk driver says temple is closed for cleaning, offers to take you to a 'local market' — a gem store paying the driver commission. Grand Palace is almost never closed to tourists.

reports.title: Gem scam near Siam Square
reports.desc: Friendly local says gems are heavily discounted 'government sale'. Items resold at home for a fraction. Do not buy gems from street strangers.

reports.title: Taxi refusing meter on Sukhumvit
reports.desc: Drivers near tourist hotels quote flat rates 3× higher. Say 'meter, please' firmly — if refused, exit and hail another taxi.

reports.title: Overpriced pad thai near Khao San Road
reports.desc: Street stalls here charge 5–10× standard Bangkok prices. Walk two streets away from Khao San for regular rates.

reports.title: Ping-pong show touts on Khao San Road at night
reports.desc: Aggressive touts outside bars offer 'free shows'. Entry may be free, drinks are not — and leaving before paying a 'minimum' leads to intimidation.

reports.title: BTS Skytrain is safe, well-lit, cheap
reports.desc: The elevated rail network is the safest way to move around Bangkok at any hour. Carry a Rabbit card (refillable, cheaper than cash fares).

reports.title: Cyclo overcharging in Old Quarter
reports.desc: Cyclo drivers agree verbally to 20,000 VND but demand 200,000 at destination. Agree on price in writing and confirm if it's per person before boarding.

reports.title: Fake Ha Long Bay tour operators in Old Quarter
reports.desc: Street agents sell 'budget' Ha Long cruises that don't match the brochure — old boats, no life vests, different itinerary. Book directly with certified operators.

reports.title: Motorbike phone grab near Hoan Kiem Lake
reports.desc: Thieves on motorbikes snatch phones from pedestrians walking close to the road. Keep phones in a pocket, not in hand, especially on the lakeside promenade.

reports.title: Lookalike hotel names in Old Quarter
reports.desc: Many hotels copy names of popular rated properties. Confirm address and check-in details via booking app — do not rely on the hotel name alone.

reports.title: Grab app is reliable and safe in Hanoi
reports.desc: Grab motorbike and car options have licensed drivers, GPS tracking, and in-app payment. Always safer than hailing a random xe ôm.

reports.title: Fake gold quality in Deira Gold Souq
reports.desc: Gold sold as 22k or 24k tested elsewhere at 14k. Reputable sellers show UAE hallmarks — insist on a receipt with karat and weight.

reports.title: Unlicensed taxis near Dubai Mall exit
reports.desc: Men in street clothes offer rides cheaper than taxis. Unlicensed, no meter, may demand cash far above quoted rate. Use the official taxi rank or Careem app.

reports.title: Timeshare pitch at JBR beach walk
reports.desc: Staff offer 'free hotel breakfast' or gift in exchange for attending a 90-minute timeshare presentation. Sessions run 4+ hours and use high-pressure tactics.

reports.title: Restaurant hidden service charges Downtown
reports.desc: 5% municipality tax + 10% service charge added to bill not shown on menu prices. Check totals before paying — overcharging beyond these rates is illegal.

reports.title: Dubai Metro is safe, AC, and punctual
reports.desc: The Red and Green Lines are spotlessly safe. Pink carriages at front are women-and-children only — use them for comfort. Off-peak is very quiet.

reports.title: Bar overcharging foreigners in Hongdae
reports.desc: Staff quote one price, charge double at the end citing 'table fee' or 'service'. Photograph menus and confirm total before ordering.

reports.title: Counterfeit K-beauty products near Myeongdong
reports.desc: Street vendors sell fake Laneige / Sulwhasoo / COSRX. Products can cause skin reactions. Buy from brand stores or authorised pharmacies (Olive Young).

reports.title: Pickpockets at Namdaemun Market
reports.desc: Crowded weekend market is prime pickpocket territory. Use a cross-body bag kept in front. Card skimming also reported at small restaurants — use cash or Samsung Pay.

reports.title: Bar harassment near Itaewon late night
reports.desc: Solo women followed from bars on the main strip after midnight. Walk in groups, use official taxis from designated stands, and avoid narrow alleys off the main road.

reports.title: Insadong is well-lit and tourist-safe at night
reports.desc: The arts district has good street lighting, CCTV coverage, and is popular with families at all hours. Good base for evening solo walks.

reports.title: Gold ring scam on Pont d'Iéna
reports.desc: Stranger 'finds' a gold ring, offers it to you, then asks for money. The ring is worthless brass. Ignore and keep walking.

reports.title: 'Deaf charity' petition near Sacré-Cœur
reports.desc: Person approaches with a clipboard claiming to collect signatures for a deaf charity, then demands a cash donation. Not a registered charity — politely decline.

reports.title: Pickpockets on Métro Line 1
reports.desc: The tourist corridor between Louvre and Champs-Élysées is the most pickpocketed stretch in Europe. Hold bag in front, use RER B from CDG rather than Line 1 from stations.

reports.title: Overpriced drinks at Champs-Élysées bars
reports.desc: Bars on the main strip charge €18–25 for a beer. Walk one or two streets off the avenue for standard Parisian café prices.

reports.title: Friendship bracelet touts at Sacré-Cœur steps
reports.desc: Men tie a bracelet on your wrist before you can refuse, then demand €20 to remove it. Keep hands in pockets on the steps — if it happens, a firm 'non' and walking away works.

reports.title: Vélib' bike sharing is safe and cheap city-wide
reports.desc: The shared bike system has 20,000 bikes. Day pass is €5, and dedicated lanes cover most of the city. Good alternative to crowded Métro during tourist season.
